title: Validation and Modification Flags
text: Validation and Modification Flags All fields, keys, and occurrences have validation and modification flags which are used to determine data should be validated or stored. In general, when the content of a field is changed, the associated validation and modification flags are set to TRUE . A field can be modified but not flagged for validation. This is the case when a field has been modified, successfully validated, but not stored. You can also have new data with both validation and modification flags set to FALSE . This situation is common but not serious. It occurs when fields have been initialized by ProcScript or in field definitions. Validation Flags Uniface checks the status of the validation flags to determine whether to activate the appropriate Validate triggers. After successful validation the validation flags are set to FALSE . You cannot set the value of validation flags using ProcScript, but you can read them using the following ProcScript functions: $fieldvalidation $keyvalidation $occvalidation $instancevalidation Modification Flags Uniface checks the status of modification tags to determine whether their associated Write triggers should be activated. After the data is stored successfully, the modification flags are set to FALSE . You can read and set the value of modification flags using one of the following ProcScript functions: $fieldcheck $keycheck $occcheck Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
